tax: Kingdom-rank branch beneath Inner Primordial Life. This branch was formerly labelled Primal; Primal is used here because it better describes native embodied life and its deep-origin descendants without implying mundane surface biology only.
ap: Material organisms have persistent bodies with tissues, mineralized or organic structure, reproductive continuity, and ecological dependence on food, water, air, light, heat, or equivalent local resources.
eco: The kingdom contains producers, decomposers, animals, sapients, giants, dragons, constructs, oozes, and monstrosities, all interacting as a biosphere rather than as planar emanations.
beh: Behavior ranges from plant tropism to sapient culture, but all members can be studied through material constraints rather than only metaphysical alignment or elemental pressure.
traitsText: Embodied matter, local reproduction or manufacture, ecological dependency, and anatomy that can leave tracks, bones, spores, shells, shed skin, tools, or ruins.
abilities: Abilities are phylum-specific: photosynthesis, digestion, venom, tool use, giantism, draconic breath, crafted animation, or monstrous specialization.
bg: Renamed from Primal to Primal to better fit the catalogue’s taxonomy.
g: Primal is the kingdom of ordinary embodied lineages in the Throat: beasts, plants, fungi, humanoids, giants, dragons, monstrosities, and other world-facing life whose ancestry is neither purely elemental, outer-planar, undead, fey, nor Far-Realm in origin.
